formulations.indian_conc_note: Framycetin sulphate 1% impregnated gauze; various sizes

dosing_bands.duration_note: Apply thin layer TDS to affected area; tulle dressing changed daily or as needed
dosing_bands.notes: Limit to small areas; avoid on large burns/wounds in children (risk of systemic aminoglycoside absorption)

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1-2 drops in affected eye QID for 7 days; may increase to hourly in severe cases then taper
dosing_bands.notes: Second-line to chloramphenicol/moxifloxacin eye drops

contraindications: Known allergy to aminoglycosides (neomycin, gentamicin cross-react)
monitoring_parameters: Signs of contact dermatitis / allergy (cross-sensitivity with neomycin)
Avoid prolonged use over large areas — risk of aminoglycoside absorption
pediatric_specific_warnings: Do NOT apply to large wound areas (burns, extensive abrasions) in children — risk of systemic aminoglycoside toxicity
Contact sensitization may occur — if rash develops around application site, discontinue
Aminoglycoside allergy cross-reactivity: if allergic to neomycin, avoid framycetin
storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; eye drops: discard 4 weeks after opening
lactation_note: Topical use on small areas compatible; avoid nipple area

therapeutic_use: Secondary bacterial skin infections with inflammation
unlicensed_note: Licensed for children; hydrocortisone 1% is mildest potency — preferred for pediatric use

dosing_bands.duration_note: Apply thin layer TDS for 7 days; if needed longer, switch to plain emollient + intermittent fusidic acid
dosing_bands.notes: Limit to 7-14 days to prevent fusidic acid resistance; hydrocortisone 1% is mildest steroid — safe for face and flexures in children

monitoring_parameters: Signs of fusidic acid resistance if repeated courses needed
pediatric_specific_warnings: Preferred combination for infected eczema in children (mildest steroid component)
Limit courses to 7-14 days to prevent fusidic acid resistance
Safe for face and flexures (hydrocortisone 1% is mild potency)
If infection not clearing in 7 days — swab for culture and sensitivity

administration.compatibility_note: Apply after emollient has absorbed (15-30 min gap)

storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; do not freeze
lactation_note: Avoid application to nipple/breast area; otherwise safe topical use

unlicensed_note: Not licensed for children; used off-label for neonatal diabetes due to KATP channel mutations and HNF1A-MODY in pediatric endocrinology

dosing_bands.duration_note: Start 0.5 mg/kg/day BD; titrate up to 1 mg/kg/day based on blood glucose; some patients need higher doses for insulin transition
dosing_bands.notes: SPECIALIST USE ONLY — used to transition from insulin in proven KATP channelopathies; crush tablet and give with feeds

dosing_bands.duration_note: Start 40 mg OD before breakfast; titrate to 80-160 mg/day in 1-2 divided doses
dosing_bands.notes: HNF1A-MODY patients are exquisitely sensitive to sulphonylureas — start very low; always with meals

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Avoid in severe renal impairment — risk of prolonged hypoglycemia

hepatic_note: Avoid in severe hepatic impairment; increased risk of hypoglycemia

interactions.effect: CYP2C9 inhibition — increased gliclazide levels → severe hypoglycemia
May mask hypoglycemia symptoms; use with caution
Antagonize hypoglycemic effect — may need dose increase

pediatric_specific_warnings: SPECIALIST USE ONLY in children — primarily for monogenic diabetes
Neonatal diabetes: crush tablet and mix with milk/formula; monitor glucose intensively during transition from insulin
HNF1A-MODY: extreme sensitivity — start with lowest possible dose
Always carry glucose/glucagon for hypoglycemia management

administration.compatibility_note: Take with breakfast; for neonates — crush tablet and mix with small amount of milk

food_instructions: Take with or immediately before meals to reduce hypoglycemia risk
storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; protect from moisture
lactation_note: May cause neonatal hypoglycemia; avoid during breastfeeding

unlicensed_note: Licensed for all ages including neonates (suppositories); ear drops licensed for all ages

formulations.indian_conc_note: Glycerol 10% in purified water; for cerumen softening

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1 infant suppository (1 g) PR once; may repeat next day if needed; not for regular use
dosing_bands.notes: Insert and retain for 15-30 min; moisten with water before insertion; works within 15-60 min

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1 child-size suppository (2 g) PR once daily as needed
dosing_bands.notes: For occasional use only; for chronic constipation use oral lactulose or PEG

dosing_bands.duration_note: 2-4 drops in affected ear BD for 3-5 days before syringing
dosing_bands.notes: Warm drops to body temperature before instillation; lie on side for 5 min after application

monitoring_parameters: Do not use suppositories long-term — address underlying cause of constipation
pediatric_specific_warnings: Suppositories are for OCCASIONAL use only — do not establish dependence
For chronic constipation, use oral laxatives (lactulose, PEG) as maintenance
Neonatal suppositories: can cut adult suppository to size or use infant-specific
May cause mild rectal irritation

administration.compatibility_note: Moisten with water before insertion; retain 15-30 min
Warm to body temperature before use

storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; suppositories may be refrigerated for easier handling
lactation_note: Safe; glycerol is a normal body metabolite

unlicensed_note: Licensed for all ages; standard anticoagulant in pediatric intensive care

formulations.indian_conc_note: Heparin sodium 1000 IU/mL; 5 mL multi-dose vial
Heparin sodium 5000 IU/mL; 5 mL multi-dose vial
Heparin sodium 10 IU/mL; for catheter patency only

dosing_bands.duration_note: Loading 75 units/kg IV over 10 min; maintenance 28 units/kg/hr; adjust to aPTT 60-85 sec
dosing_bands.notes: Neonates need HIGHER per-kg doses than older children; aPTT target 60-85 sec; check aPTT 4-6h after initiation

dosing_bands.duration_note: Loading 75 units/kg IV over 10 min; maintenance 20 units/kg/hr; adjust to aPTT 60-85 sec
dosing_bands.notes: Titrate per institutional aPTT-based nomogram; check aPTT 4-6h after each dose change

dosing_bands.duration_note: 10-100 units to fill catheter dead space; flush q8-12h or after each use
dosing_bands.notes: Use 10 IU/mL for neonates; 100 IU/mL for older children; aspirate and discard lock solution before blood sampling

hepatic_note: Use with caution in severe hepatic disease — increased bleeding risk due to reduced clotting factor synthesis

interactions.effect: Increased bleeding risk
Greatly increased hemorrhage risk
Increased bleeding risk; avoid combination if possible
May reduce heparin effect — monitor aPTT closely

monitoring_parameters: aPTT every 4-6 hours after initiation/dose change (target 60-85 sec)
Platelet count at baseline, then every 2-3 days (HIT surveillance)
Signs of bleeding (bruising, blood in stool/urine, hematoma at injection site)
Anti-Xa levels may be used instead of aPTT in some centers
black_box_warnings: Heparin-induced thrombocytopenia (HIT) can cause life-threatening arterial and venous thrombosis
Multi-dose vials contain benzyl alcohol — avoid in neonates (use preservative-free formulations)
pediatric_specific_warnings: NEONATES need higher per-kg doses due to larger antithrombin clearance
USE PRESERVATIVE-FREE heparin in neonates — benzyl alcohol toxicity risk
aPTT-based nomograms must be age-specific — adult nomograms do NOT apply
Protamine sulphate for reversal: 1 mg per 100 units of heparin given in last 2 hours
HIT is RARE in children but can occur — monitor platelets

administration.dilution: Dilute in NS or D5W; standard concentration 50-100 units/mL for infusion
administration.infusion_rate_note: Continuous IV infusion via syringe pump; loading dose over 10 minutes
administration.compatibility_note: Incompatible with many drugs — use dedicated line; incompatible with dobutamine, vancomycin, ciprofloxacin
Inject into abdominal wall; do not massage site

storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; do not freeze; preservative-free vials — use immediately
lactation_note: Not excreted in breast milk (large molecular weight); safe during breastfeeding

unlicensed_note: Licensed from 12 months of age; IAP recommends vaccination from 1 year with 2-dose schedule

formulations.indian_conc_note: Inactivated hepatitis A antigen 720 ELISA units; pediatric dose (1-15 years)
Inactivated hepatitis A antigen 1440 ELISA units; adult/adolescent dose (>15 years)
Inactivated hepatitis A antigen 80 units; 1-15 years
Inactivated hepatitis A antigen 250 U; pediatric dose; affordable Indian brand

dosing_bands.duration_note: Dose 1: 0.5 mL IM (deltoid); Dose 2: 0.5 mL IM at 6-12 months after dose 1
dosing_bands.notes: 2-dose schedule: Dose 1 at >=12 months, Dose 2 at 6-12 months later (6-18 month window acceptable); anterolateral thigh in <2yr, deltoid in >=2yr

dosing_bands.duration_note: Dose 1: 1 mL IM (deltoid); Dose 2: 1 mL IM at 6-12 months after dose 1
dosing_bands.notes: Adult formulation (1440 EU) for >=16 years; 2-dose schedule

dosing_bands.duration_note: Single dose ASAP, within 2 weeks of exposure; complete 2-dose schedule with dose 2 at 6-12 months
dosing_bands.notes: Effective as PEP if given within 14 days of exposure; for children <12 months or immunocompromised, give IG in addition

hepatic_note: Vaccine may have reduced immunogenicity in patients with chronic liver disease — still recommended (greater need for protection)
contraindications: Anaphylaxis to previous dose or vaccine component
Severe acute febrile illness (defer until recovered)

interactions.effect: May reduce vaccine immunogenicity — still recommended; consider checking anti-HAV titres after completion
Can be given concomitantly at different injection sites for PEP in high-risk contacts

monitoring_parameters: Document in vaccination record (date, dose, lot number, site)
Anti-HAV IgG titres not routinely checked after vaccination in healthy children
pediatric_specific_warnings: IAP recommends universal Hepatitis A vaccination starting at 12 months
NOT included in India's NHM/UIP — parents need to purchase privately
Can be co-administered with other vaccines at different injection sites
Second dose can be given 6-18 months after first; even if delayed, do not restart series
Injection site: anterolateral thigh for <2 years; deltoid for >=2 years

administration.compatibility_note: Shake well before use; do not freeze; can be co-administered with other vaccines at different sites
administration.storage: Store at 2-8°C; do NOT freeze — freezing destroys vaccine

storage_instructions: Store at 2-8°C (cold chain); do NOT freeze; protect from light; discard if frozen
lactation_note: Inactivated vaccine; safe during breastfeeding
reference_source: WHO Position Paper on Hepatitis A
